name: Embalse de Argos y Santuario de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza — return from Calasparra
name_original: Embalse de Argos y Santuario de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza — vuelta desde Calasparra
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Calasparra traverse a diverse landscape in the Region of Murcia, Spain. The area is characterized by four rivers, including the Segura, and features two reservoirs, creating lush lowlands that contrast with arid terrain. Cyclists can explore routes alongside these waterways, through terraced rice fields, and within the dramatic Almadenes Canyon. The region also offers mountainous terrain, providing varied gradients for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Calasparra The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Argos y Santuario de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza — return from Calasparra, a 24.7 miles (39.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects the Argos Reservoir with the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hope. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Calasparra – Duck Lake loop from Calasparra, a difficult 24.4 miles (39.3 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain starting and ending in Calasparra. Local touring cyclists also love the Greenway of the Northwest – Cehegín Old Town Monuments loop from Cementerio Municipal de Calasparra, a 44.1 miles (70.9 km) trail leading through dedicated paths and historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Calasparra is defined by its rivers, reservoirs, rice fields, and Greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more leisurely rides. The routes in Calasparra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 7 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Calasparra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Calasparra?
A: Komoot currently lists 7 touring cycling routes around Calasparra. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from challenging climbs to more leisurely rides, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Calasparra?
A: Touring cycling in Calasparra offers a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll encounter routes alongside four rivers, including the Segura, and two reservoirs, creating lush lowlands. The area is also famous for its terraced rice fields, which provide unique green vistas, especially when flooded. Dramatic rocky landscapes of the Almadenes Canyon and varied mountainous terrain are also prominent features.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Calasparra?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available for touring cyclists in Calasparra. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27627208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calasparra – Duck Lake loop from Calasparra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering varied terrain starting and ending in Calasparra. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38220828" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argos Reservoir loop from Hotel Argos&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a scenic circuit around the reservoir.

Q: What historical and cultural sites can I visit along touring cycling routes in Calasparra?
A: Calasparra's touring cycling routes can lead you to several significant historical and cultural sites. A notable destination is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt;, carved into a cave near the Segura River. The region also boasts ancient sites like Villa Vieja, Calasparra's Castle, and the Abrigos del Pozo with its 8,000-year-old cave paintings, offering a glimpse into the area's rich past.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks or viewpoints to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt; (Almadenes Canyon) is a protected natural area with stunning rocky landscapes and riverine forests, offering trails to various viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cañón de Almadenes&lt;/a&gt;. The Las Lomas observation deck provides panoramic views of the famous rice fields. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Calasparra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Calasparra are predominantly challenging, with 6 out of 7 routes rated as 'difficult' by the komoot community. These routes often involve significant elevation changes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017227831/embalse-de-argos-y-santuario-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-esperanza-return-from-calasparra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Argos y Santuario de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza — return from Calasparra&lt;/a&gt;, which has over 450 meters of elevation gain. There is also one 'moderate' route available for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there routes suitable for families with children?
A: While many routes in Calasparra are rated as 'difficult', the region's Greenways, such as sections of the 'Greenway of the Northwest', often provide flatter, dedicated paths that can be more suitable for families. These paths are typically converted from disused railway lines, offering a safer and more relaxed cycling experience away from traffic. Always check the specific route details for elevation and terrain before heading out with children.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Calasparra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Calasparra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 7 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, which includes scenic river valleys, unique rice fields, and challenging mountainous sections. The integration of natural beauty with historical sites like the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hope also contributes to a highly enjoyable and memorable experience.

Q: Are touring cycling routes in Calasparra suitable for dogs?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary, many natural areas and Greenways in Calasparra are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. However, it's always recommended to check local signage or specific route descriptions for any restrictions, especially in protected natural areas or private lands, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Where can I find parking near touring cycling trailheads in Calasparra?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Calasparra, particularly near the starting points of popular routes or within the town itself. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3957026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Northwest – Cehegín Old Town Monuments loop from Cementerio Municipal de Calasparra&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking facilities near municipal areas or designated trailheads. It's advisable to check maps or route descriptions for specific parking suggestions at your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there options for public transport access to touring cycling routes in Calasparra?
A: Public transport options to access touring cycling routes in Calasparra are limited, as is common in many rural Spanish regions. While there might be bus services connecting Calasparra to larger towns, direct access to specific trailheads via public transport with a bicycle can be challenging. Planning your route to start and end in Calasparra or a nearby town with better transport links is often the most practical approach.
tips: Built in 1970, the Argos reservoir is located in the Sierra de la Puerta and situated on the Argos river, a tributary of the Segura. A beautiful picture while we ride along itinerary 6 of the Ciclosegura route in the Murcia region.

The Sanctuary is located in a place 6 km by road from the town. The hermitage houses two images of the Virgen de la Esperanza, patron saint of Calasparra, who are worshiped together. The primitive hermitage (XVII) was in one of the caves on the Segura river, a natural shelter for shepherds, to which new rooms and buildings have been added with the expansion of the cult. The creation of terraces up to the river has been succeeded in modern times by the creation of service areas: parking lots, restaurant, hotel, etc.
Nice views from the viewpoint to the Segura river and the rice fields. Access is paved to the viewpoint. Without a doubt, it is worth visiting if we are in the area.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope
